seo.title: Building Voice Agents with AG2 + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with AG2 + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Voice Agents built with AG2 enable real-time multi-agent conversations powered by speech-to-text, LLM reasoning, and text-to-speech pipelines. With sub-second latency requirements, these agents handle dynamic group discussions but are prone to off-topic spirals, factual errors, and unsafe outputs. Vex integration provides essential guardrails to ensure response appropriateness, conversation coherence, and safety in high-risk deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with AG2
architectureOverview.description:
Voice Agents in AG2 follow a real-time pipeline: audio input is transcribed via speech-to-text (e.g., Whisper), fed into AG2's group chat agents for reasoning, then converted back to speech via text-to-speech (e.g., ElevenLabs). AG2's enhanced group chat handles speaker selection and consensus, but requires message hooks for latency monitoring and safety checks. Vex registers as a hook to verify each agent response before TTS, ensuring sub-second budgets while filtering harmful content.

The architecture supports low-latency by using streaming APIs and async agent execution. Group chats simulate turn-taking with speaker selection logic, but common failures include bias toward dominant speakers or hallucinated facts derailing conversations. Vex mitigates these by validating coherence and appropriateness at each message boundary.
architectureOverview.diagram:
Mic → [STT: Whisper] → [AG2 Group Chat + Vex Hooks] → [TTS: ElevenLabs] → Speaker
                  ↓
             Latency Monitor (<500ms)
                  ↓
             Safety Filter → Block/Reject

risks.name: Agent conversation spiraling off-topic
risks.description: Voice agents in group chats deviate from user intent due to unconstrained multi-turn reasoning
risks.mitigation: Vex coherence checks on conversation history and topic relevance

risks.name: Latency budget violations
risks.description: STT-LLM-TTS pipeline exceeds sub-second requirements, breaking real-time interaction
risks.mitigation: Vex timeout enforcement and response truncation

risks.name: Safety violations in speech output
risks.description: Agents generate harmful, biased, or inappropriate TTS content
risks.mitigation: Vex safety filtering before TTS synthesis

risks.name: Speaker selection bias
risks.description: Group chat favors certain agents, leading to imbalanced or incorrect consensus
risks.mitigation: Vex fairness validation on speaker turns and content diversity

implementation.title: Basic AG2 Voice Agent Group
implementation.description: Set up core AG2 group chat with STT/TTS integration for real-time voice conversations
implementation.code:
from ag2 import GroupChat, GroupChatManager
from ag2.agent import AssistantAgent
import whisper
import elevenlabs

# STT and TTS clients
stt_model = whisper.load_model('base')
voice_client = elevenlabs.VoiceClient()

listener_agent = AssistantAgent(name='Listener', llm_config={'model': 'gpt-4o-mini'})
reasoner_agent = AssistantAgent(name='Reasoner', llm_config={'model': 'gpt-4o-mini'})

# Vex hook placeholder
def vex_hook(message):
    return True  # Will integrate Vex

groupchat = GroupChat(agents=[listener_agent, reasoner_agent], messages=[], max_round=10)
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat, llm_config={'model': 'gpt-4o-mini'})

# Voice loop
def voice_loop():
    audio = record_audio()  # Real-time mic input
    text = stt_model.transcribe(audio)['text']
    response = manager.initiate_chat(listener_agent, message=text, message_hook=vex_hook)
    audio_out = voice_client.generate(response)
    play_audio(audio_out)

implementation.title: Real-time Latency Monitoring
implementation.description: Track STT → LLM → TTS pipeline timing to enforce sub-second budgets

implementation.title: Speaker Selection Logic
implementation.description: Implement fair turn-taking for multi-agent voice conversations
implementation.code:
from ag2 import select_speaker

class VoiceSpeakerSelector:
    def __init__(self):
        self.last_speaker = None
        self.speaker_counts = {}
    
    def select_speaker(self, groupchat: GroupChat):
        # Avoid consecutive same speaker
        candidates = [agent for agent in groupchat.agents 
                     if agent != self.last_speaker]
        
        # Weighted by expertise or recency
        speaker = select_speaker(groupchat, selector_method='auto')
        if speaker in candidates:
            self.last_speaker = speaker
            self.speaker_counts[speaker.name] = self.speaker_counts.get(speaker.name, 0) + 1
        return speaker

# Use in GroupChatManager
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat, speaker_selector=VoiceSpeakerSelector())

implementation.title: Streaming TTS Integration
implementation.description: Enable partial TTS rendering for lowest possible latency
implementation.code:
import asyncio
from elevenlabs import stream

async def stream_voice_response(response_generator):
    async for chunk in response_generator:
        # Stream partial LLM responses
        if chunk:
            audio_chunk = elevenlabs.generate(chunk, stream=True)
            stream(audio_chunk)

# Integrate with AG2 streaming
def streaming_chat_loop():
    async def run():
        response_stream = manager.stream_chat(listener_agent, message=text)
        await stream_voice_response(response_stream)
    asyncio.run(run())
